title: Miguel O'Hara (Earth-928)
content:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Miguel O'Hara}} {{CharacterTabber}} {{Character |name = Spider-Man |image = GH0khxnXkAABQR1.jpg|Miguel O'Hara MiguelHDR.jpg|Spider-Man |fullname = Miguel O'Hara |alias = '''Codenames'''
Spider-Man''[[Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse]]''
'''Editorial Names'''
Spider-Man 2099

Only Spider-Man without a Sense of Humor |counterpart = * [[Miguel O'Hara|Miguel O'Hara]] * [[Spider-People|Spider-Man]] |universe = [[Earth-928]] |location = Nueva York |identity = Secret |status = Alive |born = |age = 35 |affiliation = * [[Spider-Society]] * [[Lyla]] * [[Alchemax]] (formerly) |marital Status = Single |relatives = [[Gabriella O'Hara]] (alternate daughter/adoptive daughter; deceased) |species = Human (enhanced) |ethnicity = {{Citizenship|MEX}}
{{Citizenship|IRL}} |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA}} |gender = Male |skin color = Brown |eyes = Dark Brown
Red (temporarily) |hair = Black |height = 206 cm (6'9) |weight = |appearances = * ''[[Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse]]'' ([[List of Post-Credit Scenes|post-credits scene]]) * ''[[Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse]]'' * ''[[Spider-Man: Beyond the Spider-Verse]]'' (upcoming) |voice actor = [[Oscar Isaac]] |actor =}}{{Quote|My name is Miguel O'Hara. I'm [[Earth-928|this dimension]]'s one and only Spider-Man. At least I [[Spider-Society|was]]. But I'm not like [[Spider-People|the others]]. I don't always like what I have to do. But I know I have to be the one to do it. I've given up too much to stop now.|[[Miguel O'Hara (Earth-928)|Spider-Man]] briefly sharing his background.|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse}} '''Miguel O'Hara''' is the superhero '''Spider-Man''' of the year [[2090s/Earth-928|2099]]. He is the leader of the multiversal [[Spider-Society]] which strives to keep the "canon" intact. As Spider-Man, Miguel would have a lot of adventures along with his Artificial Intelligence, [[LYLA]]. With both of them protecting [[Earth-928|Nueva York]], sometime after becoming Spider-Man, Miguel would discover the [[Multiverse|multiverse]] and decide to monitor it since then alongside LYLA. Realizing that the events of the [[Super-Collider]] on [[Earth-1610]] produced multiversal rifts that cause [[Anomalies|various villains]] to be stranded in universes they don't belong to. Spider-Man would find the Spider-Society as a way to prevent the multiverse from collapsing as well as send the villains back to their respective universes. After having protected the multiverse from various threats, Miguel would decide to replace his counterpart from an alternate universe to raise his daughter after that Miguel died after being robbed by a thief. However, this event would cause the destruction of this universe as well as its inhabitants. Being severely traumatized, Miguel began to work excessively to the point that he would leave behind the sarcastic and happy man he once was. After discovering about the [[Canon Events]] and their meaning for the lives of the [[Spider-Totems|Spider-People]], Miguel would become stricter with the Spider-Society, forbidding all members from visiting the [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]] of [[Earth-1610]] for finding out that the latter was an [[Anomalies|anomaly]]. Sometime later, Miguel and the rest of the Spider-Society would come into conflict with the Spot as well as Miles Morales when the latter tried to save his father once he discovered the latter's impending death. Even though he tries to talk to him at first, Miles doesn't give him the chance, making Miguel reveal the origin of Miles as an anomaly. Miles ends up escaping to what he thinks is his universe. Miguel, along with [[Jessica Drew (Earth-332)|Jessica]] and [[Ben Reilly|Ben]], decide to find Miles to prevent him from saving his [[Jefferson Morales (Earth-1610)|dad]] and accidentally causing the demise of his universe in the process. ==Biography== ====Becoming Spider-Man==== At some point, Miguel O'Hara would gain super-human abilities including retractable talons, fangs, and enhanced strength. He'd use these abilities to become the superhero named Spider-Man. With help from his holographic companion [[LYLA]], he'd learn about the multiverse and investigate how it's run.'''' ===Forming the Spider-Society=== ====Visiting Other Universes==== From their base in the future, Miguel and his holographic companion [[LYLA]] monitored the activation of the [[Super-Collider]] and meeting of the [[Spider-Gang]], anticipating the potential collapse of the [[multiverse]]. Miguel left for about two hours and ended up missing the incident's conclusion and caught up to the events by Lyla. [[File:Spider-Men (E-67) 001.png|thumb|256x256px|left]] Upon his return, he found that the prototype of their "gizmo", made to help him make an "autonomous multiverse jump", was completed. Miguel immediately put it on and decided to jump to [[Earth-67]]. Once there, Miguel landed in front of what he thought was that dimension's [[Peter Parker (Earth-67)|Spider-Man]] (unaware that it's actually Charles Cameo, the imposter Spider-Man) and immediately tried to convince him to go with him but instead got into a pointing argument with him.''[[Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse]]'' Sometime after, Miguel began recruiting more Spider-Men as a way to keep the multiverse safe from any threat after he realizes that the Collider Crisis stranded several villains in other universes. For that, Miguel would visit [[Earth-616]], [[Earth-404]] among other universes to recruit the Spider-Men of these universes to help him in his missions of sending the displaced villains back to their home reality, along the way Miguel would become a competent leader for the team along with [[Jessica Drew (Earth-404)|Jessica Drew]].'''' ===The Canon Accident=== Some time later after founding the Spider-Society, Miguel would find [[List of Unidentified Universes|a world]] where an [[Miguel O'Hara (Alternate 928)|alternate version]] of himself had a [[Gabriella O'Hara|daughter]] and lived a happy and peaceful domestic life. After discovering this universe, Miguel would secretly look at this universe secretly from all the members of the newly founded Spider-Society, beginning to feel a little envy and sadness after seeing how a version of him could be happy if he had not become Spider-Man. However, his life will change drastically once his counterpart had been cruelly murdered, Miguel under good intentions decided to take his place in order to raise his now orphaned daughter. After this, Miguel would begin to spend more time in his counterpart's universe enjoying being a father and his family to the point that he no longer returned to his universe or related as much to the members of the Spider-Society with Miguel apparently, leaving his life as Spider-Man behind to be a family man. [[File:Miguel loose his daugther.png|thumb|303x303px|Miguel in devastation after witnessing his daughter die while [[Peter Parker (Earth-616)|Peter B. Parker]] watches helplessly.]] Despite how peaceful his newfound life as a father was, things would begin to change. The universe began to fall apart together with its inhabitants realizing what Miguel was going through, and would call some members of the Spider-Society, in order to save the people he loves. However, not even with the help of the [[Peter Parker (Earth-616)|original Spider-Man]] did they manage to achieve this goal, since that entire universe is destroyed while Miguel watches as his daughter disappears in his arms. After that Miguel stays looking at his arms in horror after he sees how everything he ever loved disappeared as Peter B. consoled him for his loss.'''' ====The Aftermath of the Accident==== {{Biography Quote|Miles, we all want to live the life we wish we had. Believe me, I have tried. And the harder I tried, the more damage I did. You can't have it all, kid. Being Spider-Man is a sacrifice. That's the job. That's what you signed up for.|Miguel O'Hara to [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]]|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse}} After these events, Miguel would feel abandoned and full of guilt after that event, while little by little he begins to distance himself and become more strict with the Spider-Society. Wanting to make up for his mistake and find out what happened, Miguel began to investigate the multiverse more than ever as a way to know how to properly protect it and prevent similar events from occurring. In his studies, Spider-Man discovered the Web of Life and Destiny, a small pocket found in the multiverse which seemed to be the established timeline of canonical events for spider-like heroes throughout the multiverse who couldn't be interrupted, so that this universe would not be destroyed after finding out about those events Miguel would be strict with those Canon Events deciding along with the Spider-Society to make sure canon events occurred. He also discovered that the collider event on Earth-1610 made the spider radioactive that intended to bite Miles Morales from Earth-42 and turning him into Spider-Man, ended up being transported to Earth-1610 and biting Miles Morales from that world and turning him into Spider-Man in his place. Coming to the conclusion that Miles was an anomaly and that his presence could be a danger to the multiverse, Miguel would give the rule to all members of the spider-society, especially those who were related to Miles (such as Peter B. or Peni Parker) not to visit him or try to get him on the team. Even so, Miguel promised as a reward that he would not tell Miles the truth that he is an Anomaly as well as the Canon Event that was about to take place in his universe.[[Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse|Spider-Man: ''Across the Spider-Verse'']] ===Meeting Gwen Stacy=== Miguel would receive news that there was a canon displacement on [[Earth-65]] involving a variant of [[Adriano Tumino|the vulture]]. Miguel would arrive in the universe's Guggenheim Museum and immediately engaged the anomaly in battle, briefly knocking him out. Miguel would introduce himself to the resident [[Gwendolyn Stacy (Earth-65)|Spider-Woman]] of the universe before being immediately knocked out by the Vulture due to his lack of Spider-Sense. The two Spider-People would team up and battle the Vulture before Miguel reluctantly decided to call in back up from [[Jessica Drew (Earth-332)|Jessica Drew]]. The 3 Spider-People then managed to take on the Vulture and succeed in apprehending him as well as preventing a helicopter crash to occur over the civilians in the museum. Before leaving the universe however, Miguel would notice Captain Stacy pulling a gun on his own daughter. With a bit of his fatherly instincts kicking in, he immediately restrained Captain Stacy in an electric cage. Realizing there was no other way to fix this situation, Miguel recruited Gwen into the Spider-Society by throwing her a watch. Upon letting Gwen into the society, Miguel would tell her about the concept of the "canon events" including the one surrounding her death often occurring from romantic relationships with Spider-Man. He'd also instruct her not to visit Miles Morales under any circumstances due to his status as anomaly, much to her dismay. ==Personality== {{Biography Quote|You always have to call me out? It's just really frustrating and that bums me out.|Miguel O'Hara to [[LYLA]]|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}} Miguel was initially shown to have a very sarcastic attitude. He also spoke coolly about things, even when [[LYLA|Lyla]] tells him that his multiverse-hopping device could kill him. He also displays a hyperactive personality that doesn't think of anything before acting sometimes getting carried away when someone insults him or something similar. Miguel also proved to be someone excited in the realm of science, creating the [[Multiversal Gizmo|Gizmo]] and the [[Spider-Society]] initially to defend the multiverse from any threat. Miguel is a perfectionist, always looking for order and structure, and maintaining stability within the multiverse. He envisions his morals and leads the [[Spider-Society|Spider Society]]. {{Biography Quote|I'm trying to hold a serious adult conversation here."
"You know, you're the only Spider-Man who isn't funny. We’re supposed to be funny.|Miguel O'Hara and [[Peter Parker (Earth-616)|Peter B. Parker]]|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse}} After his failure to prevent the destruction of an alternate dimension, Miguel was left emotionally scarred by the event, becoming an overworked leader of the Spider-Society that tried to make sure that all canon events of the different universes occurred at all cost. After that, he has become very serious and reserved, easily stressed out and very judgmental of all newcomers that tried to join his group. It also implied that Miguel doesn't feel very proud of the things he does. Sometimes even doubting if what he is doing is right but according to him if he doesn't do these things nobody would do it. That shows that following the destruction of that universe, Miguel became obsessive to protect the Multiverse by any means leaving the happy and sarcastic man he once was. Although, despite being serious and reserved, Miguel at his core does care about helping people in need, but lost sight of who Spider-Man is suppose to be. Also, Miguel did promise Peter B. that he wouldn't tell [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]] the truth about his anomaly status or capture him. However, Miguel secretly intended to break half of his promise, as he attempted to capture Miles, but tried to keep the other part of the promise. Unfortunately, Miguel let his temper get the better of him as he broke his word to Peter. B and told Miles the truth about everything. Miguel also isn't afraid to use others in order to achieve his goals as he used Gwen and Peter B. to get to Miles in order to capture him, due to their relationship with him, as he kicked Gwen out of the Spider-Society and brushed Peter B. off, after he was done with them. Miguel resents Miles Morales for disrupting canon events in his own universe, despite the fact that other-wordly factors are to blame, such as Alchemax releasing Earth-42's spider or [[Jonathan Ohnn]] sending the Spider to Earth-1610 in the first place. Also, Miguel's other reason for resenting Miles is that he blames him for everything that has happened with the Multiverse and went to the point of banning Spider-People from visiting Miles, as seen with Gwen who was still allowed to travel to Hobie's dimension. Despite his resentment towards Miles, Miguel did make an effort to be polite to Miles by explaining his beliefs, past and canon event. Miguel may have explained this to Miles in an attempt to reason with him. Because of this, he was blinded to the latter's potential as Miles later revealed he intentionally ran so he could lure Miguel and the others away from the base to have access to the transporter. Miguel was infuriated by underestimating Miles. ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== {{Biography Quote|Oh, he's like a ninja-vampire Spider-Man, but a good guy.|[[Gwendolyn Stacy (Earth-65)|Gwen Stacy]] to [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]]|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse}} *'''Spider physiology''': Unlike other spider-men, Miguel gained his superpowers trying to reverse his body's chemistry to before he was drugged by rapture (a highly addictive drug). His Experiment was sabotaged by his former supervisor who enlaced spider genetics with Miguel's base line. Miguel survived, gaining instant Spider Abilities such as vampire-like fangs and sharp talons at his toes and fingertips. It's also hinted that Miguel needs to consistently dose himself with artificial "radioactive spider bites", but the reason for so has not been addressed yet. Unlike other spider-men, Miguel does not have the spider sense. **'''Superhuman strength''': Miguel possesses enhanced strength, allowing him to lift heavy objects and overpower his opponents in combat. **'''Superhuman durability:''' Miguel's body has enhanced durability, allowing him to withstand physical impacts and attacks to a greater extent than an ordinary human. He can endure blows, falls, and other forms of physical trauma without sustaining severe injuries. **'''Superhuman speed:''' Miguel possesses enhanced speed, enabling him to move at incredibly fast velocities. This enhanced speed allows him to outpace regular humans and react swiftly in combat situations. **'''Superhuman agility''': He has incredible agility, reflexes, and acrobatic skills, enabling him to move swiftly and gracefully in combat and navigate through various environments effortlessly. **'''Regenerative healing factor''': Miguel's accelerated healing factor allows him to recover from injuries at an increased rate, healing wounds and broken bones more rapidly than a normal human. **'''Wallcrawling''': Like other Spider-People, Miguel has the ability to cling to and crawl on walls and ceilings, mainly using his talons, giving him enhanced mobility and the ability to navigate vertical surfaces with ease. **'''Talons:''' Miguel is shown to have extremely sharp talons that can rip through metal and spider webs. He can summon his talons on command and retract them by touching his own skin. Miguel's talons can slice through guns, swords, and flesh. **'''Venomous Fangs:''' Miguel's fangs can deliver a venomous bite, capable of temporarily immobilizing his opponents. ===Abilities=== {{Biography Quote|The [[Spider-Society|whole thing]] was his idea.|[[Gwendolyn Stacy (Earth-65)|Gwen Stacy]] to [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]]|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse}} *'''Genius-Level Intellect:''' Miguel is incredibly intelligent and possesses a genius-level intellect. He is a skilled scientist and has expertise in various fields such as genetics, biochemistry, and robotics. His intelligence often aids him in solving complex problems and creating advanced technology. **'''Technological Aptitude:''' As a scientist and futurist, Miguel has a deep understanding of advanced technology. He is proficient in using and manipulating various high-tech gadgets and inventions, often incorporating them into his crime-fighting arsenal. **'''Investigative Skills:''' Miguel possesses sharp investigative skills, allowing him to gather information, analyze data, and uncover secrets. He has a keen eye for detail and can piece together clues to solve mysteries and uncover the truth. **'''Multilingualism:''' Miguel is fluent in multiple languages, including English, Spanish, and Japanese. This linguistic skill proves valuable in communicating with individuals from different backgrounds and navigating various situations. *'''Skilled Combatant:''' Miguel has received extensive combat training, making him a skilled hand-to-hand combatant. He is proficient in multiple fighting styles and martial arts, allowing him to hold his own in battles even without relying solely on his superhuman abilities. ==Equipment== *'''[[Spider-Man Suit]]''': A special futuristic suit used by Miguel O'Hara to conceal his identity while operating as the superhero Spider-Man. It seems to be digital or holographic instead of spandex or metal armor like most Spider suits. *Smart Web: Miguel's suit is able to generate red laser web lines, called "Smart Webs", from the red areas of the suit. Miguel is only shown using Smart Webs to immobilize targets, so it is unknown if he can swing from them or not. *Cape: Miguel's suit can generate a retractable red cape similar in design to a spider web, which allows him to glide in the air. *'''[[Multiversal Gizmo]]''': The Multiversal Gizmo, created by [[LYLA]], allows Miguel to maintain stability in alternate universes and travel to other dimensions with ease. ==Relationships== ===Allies=== *[[LYLA]] - Creation, Friend and Assistant *[[Gabriella O'Hara]] - Alternate Reality Daughter * [[Spider-Society]] - Teammates and Subordinates ** Parker/Spider-Man]] - Former Subordinate turned Enemy ** [[Pavitr Prabhakar|Pavitr Prabhakar/Spider-Man]] - Former Subordinate turned Enemy ** [[Hobart Brown (Earth-138)|Hobart Brown/Spider-Punk]] - Former Subordinate turned Enemy ** [[Margo Kess|Margo Kess/Spider-Byte]] - Former Subordinate and Former Tech Support turned Enemy ** [[Peter Parker (Earth-90214)|Peter Parker/Spider-Man Noir]] ** [[Peni Parker]] - Former Subordinate turned Enemy ** [[Peter Porker|Peter Porker/Spider-Ham]] ==Gallery== Miguel O'Hara 001.png|Miguel's credit scene in 'Into The Spider-Verse' File:Lyla and Miguel 001.png|Miguel and Lyla in 'Into The Spider-Verse' Miguel in Earth-67 001.png Spider-Men (E-67) 001.png File:Miguel O'Hara 01.jpg File:Miguel O'Hara (Earth-928) from Spider-Man Across the Spider-Verse 001.png ==Trivia== *In the comics, and likely in this franchise, [[w:c:marvel:Miguel O'Hara|Miguel O'Hara]] is Spider-Man 2099 from [[w:c:marvel:Earth-928|Earth-928]], a possible future of the mainstream dimension [[w:c:marvel:Earth-616|Earth-616]]. * According to [[Bob Persichetti]], Miguel wasn't the only candidate for the post-credits Spider-Man that would hint at the possibilities for the future of the franchise. The decision to choose Miguel stemmed from the director and creators' perception that the "fans were very vocally disappointed to learn that O'Hara wasn't part of the [[Spider-Gang|central ''Into the Spider-Verse'' team]]."[https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2018/12/spider-man-into-the-spider-verse-end-of-credits-who-is-miguel-ohara Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse: The End of Credits Scene, Explained] | Vanity Fair ==References== {{DEFAULTSORT:O'Hara, Miguel}} [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man: Beyond the Spider-Verse characters]] [[Category:Male characters]] [[Category:Americans]] [[Category:Mexicans]] [[Category:Irishes]] [[Category:Heroes]] [[Category:Spider-People]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Spider-Society Members]] [[Category:Miguel O'Hara]] [[Category:Earth-928]] [[Category:Swarthy Skin]] [[Category:Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder]] [[Category:Depression]]

I don't always like what I have to do. But I know I have to be the one to do it. I've given up too much to stop now.|[[Miguel O'Hara (Earth-928)|Spider-Man]] briefly sharing his background.|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse
